mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133755k as of Oct 2012 shows ongoing recovery
failure_condition: Payroll growth accelerates above 200k/month but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% and inflation (CPI) grows slower than 2.5% annually, the real interest rate is deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 231.751 as of Oct 2012
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered periods, or correlation between real rate and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice earnings forecasts downward.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines > 1% but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains stable or declining, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and profit margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE is flat or declining
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.03 and UNRATE at 7.9% as of Oct 2012
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with labor slack absorption
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 900k annualized units while real GDP growth remains positive, indicating sustained residential investment and construction employment that supports aggregate demand and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 900k annualized AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 872k and GDPC1 at 13616.2B as of Aug-Sep 2012
regime_hint: housing recovery with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation-adjusted real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus 12-month CPI growth) turns positive and remains above 0.5%, the monetary stance shifts toward tightening, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 231.751 as of Oct 2012
failure_condition: Real rate exceeds 0.5% but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered periods, or the real rate signal shows Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while payroll employment remains flat or negative, signaling simultaneous supply contraction and labor shedding that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS is flat or negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.03 and PAYEMS at 133755k as of Oct 2012
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year window.
